. It serves as a reference for development, deployment

the Build and Design phase from SOW. Below is an example>

The deployment of the integration interfaces will follow a structured progression across three environments: DEV, UAT, and PROD. Initial development and unit testing will occur in the DEV environment, followed by system integration testing (SIT) and user acceptance testing in the UAT environment. Once the solution passes all validation checkpoints, it will be deployed to the PROD environment for live use.
nsure all components, including testing the current workflows and not only the updates ones.
It is recommended to simulate high-volume and edge-case scenarios during SIT.
uch as processing 100+ HL7 messages per batch and testing invalid payloads to confirm error logging and alert mechanisms work correctly.
Ensure that both positive and negative test cases are covered and include stakeholders from both development and business teams in test case review and defect triaging.
A formal sign-off from the integration lead and QA is required before moving to production deployment.
The existing interface specifications will be backed up to be rolled back if needed.
Add interface related assumptions from the SOW. Add more if identified during design workshop
